_cite.reason: 4c: relabel unlabeled workspace-analysis to labeled analysis (g17 §8); every binding sentence judged WORKSPACE_ANALYSIS

prior.content_md:
*This section describes the Green Ship Programme structure as publicly documented on the MPA MSGI overview page. Revised parameters introduced by Circular No. 12 of 2024 are not reproduced here because the circular's PDF text was not machine-readable in the source content provided. This is a confirmed research gap.*

**Programme structure (prior to and as context for the revision):**

The Green Ship Programme, as described on the MPA MSGI page, provides incentives to owners of Singapore-registered ships that meet specified energy efficiency standards. Vessels receiving GSP notation are recognised for exceeding IMO Energy Efficiency Design Index (EEDI) baselines or equivalent energy efficiency criteria. The programme is voluntary for shipowners; there is no mandate compelling enrolment.

*Source: Maritime Singapore Green Initiative, MPA. https://www.mpa.gov.sg/maritime-singapore/sustainability/maritime-singapore-green-initiative [Tier 2]*

The Green Port Programme, a parallel MSGI component distinct from the GSP, provides port dues rebates of up to 50% (figure cited in prior public MPA communications — current rate post-Circular No. 12 revision **not confirmed from source content provided**) for ocean-going vessels calling at Singapore that use qualifying fuels (LNG, methanol, ammonia, hydrogen, or vessels meeting defined emissions thresholds). The GPP is not revised by Circular No. 12 of 2024 based on the circular's title; however, this cannot be confirmed without reading the full PDF.

*Source: Maritime Singapore Green Initiative, MPA. https://www.mpa.gov.sg/maritime-singapore/sustainability/maritime-singapore-green-initiative [Tier 2]*

**What Circular No. 12 of 2024 revised:** Title confirms it is a revision to the GSP for Singapore-registered ships. Specific revised criteria, thresholds, eligibility changes, and any new notation categories are **not confirmed from source content provided**. The workspace's ocean team must obtain the PDF to determine whether vessels used by its carriers have changed qualification status.

**Confirmed research gap:** The specific revised programme parameters introduced by MPA Shipping Circular No. 12 of 2024 are not available in machine-readable form from the source content provided. This brief cannot reproduce or interpret those parameters. Reading the primary PDF is the only remediation.

---
